skill_name: Reduced Relative Clauses with Past Participle (The book written by…)

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The letter ___ (write) by the manager was sent to all staff members this morning.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'writing' signals an active meaning, but the letter did not write anything — a past participle is required for the passive reduced relative
'that writing' incorrectly combines the relative pronoun 'that' with a present participle, producing a grammatically broken structure
'that written' incorrectly retains the relative pronoun 'that' before the past participle; in a reduced relative clause, 'that' is omitted

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The cake ___ (make) by Anna for the party was completely eaten within an hour.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'making' implies an active role — the cake was not making anything; the passive past participle is needed
'that made' is an error where the student uses a full relative clause structure but omits the auxiliary 'was', making it grammatically incorrect
'was made' introduces a finite verb form that turns the reduced relative into a full finite clause, breaking the noun-phrase structure

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The report ___ (prepare) by the finance team must be submitted before Friday afternoon.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'preparing' signals active meaning — the report did not prepare anything; the passive past participle is required
'that was preparing' uses a full relative clause with active continuous meaning, which contradicts the passive context — the team prepared the report, not the report
'that prepare' uses the base form in a relative clause with no auxiliary, which is grammatically incorrect in any tense

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The photographs ___ (take) by the professional photographer at the event were displayed in the entrance hall.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'taking' implies the photographs were actively doing the taking, which is nonsensical; the passive past participle is required
'that were taking' is a full active relative clause — it implies the photographs were taking something, which is semantically impossible
simple past 'took' introduces a finite verb that creates a run-on clause without a conjunction, making the sentence grammatically incorrect

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The proposal ___ (submit) by the two junior architects last month has now been approved by the board.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'submitting' implies the proposal was actively submitting something, which is semantically impossible and grammatically wrong for a passive reduced relative
'that submitting' combines a relative pronoun with a present participle without an auxiliary verb, producing an ungrammatical structure
'which submitted' is a full relative clause with active meaning — it incorrectly implies the proposal itself performed the action of submitting

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The new road ___ (build) by the construction company last year has already developed several serious cracks.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'building' gives the road an active role — roads do not build things; the passive past participle is required
'that built' uses an active relative clause, wrongly implying the road performed the building action
'was built' introduces a finite passive verb that creates a second independent clause, making the sentence a run-on without a coordinating conjunction

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The medicine ___ (prescribe) by the doctor needs to be taken twice a day with food.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'prescribing' implies the medicine is actively prescribing something to someone, which is semantically absurd and grammatically incorrect for a passive reduced relative
'which prescribing' combines a relative pronoun with a present participle without an auxiliary, producing an ungrammatical fragment
'that was prescribing' is a full active relative clause implying the medicine performed the prescribing action, which contradicts the real-world relationship

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The research findings ___ (publish) by the university team earlier this year have attracted significant attention from health professionals worldwide.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'publishing' implies the findings were actively publishing something, which is semantically impossible; a passive past participle is required to show the findings were the object of the publishing action
'that were publishing' is a full active relative clause incorrectly suggesting the findings performed the publishing, reversing the logical agent-patient relationship
perfect participle 'having published' signals an active completed action by the subject — it would mean the findings themselves completed a publishing action, which is logically impossible

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The budget cuts ___ (announce) by the director at last week's meeting have caused considerable concern among the project managers.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'announcing' gives the budget cuts an active role — cuts do not announce things; the passive past participle is required to show the cuts were the subject of the announcement
'which announcing' combines a relative pronoun with a bare present participle without an auxiliary, producing an ungrammatical and incoherent structure
'having been announcing' is a perfect continuous passive participle implying an ongoing action over time, which contradicts the one-time event of a formal announcement at a specific meeting

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The complaints ___ (receive) by the customer service department over the past two weeks clearly indicate that the new system needs to be improved.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'receiving' implies the complaints were actively receiving something, which is semantically impossible; the past participle is required to show the complaints were received by the department
'that received' uses an active relative clause suggesting the complaints performed the action of receiving, which reverses the correct agent-patient relationship

present passive participle 'being received' signals an ongoing action happening at the moment of speaking, which is inconsistent with the time frame 'over the past two weeks' that refers to a completed accumulation of complaints

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The email ___ (send) to all staff members yesterday explained the new office rules clearly.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'sending' signals an active reduced relative, but the email did not send itself — a passive past participle is required
'that sending' incorrectly combines a relative pronoun with a present participle, producing an ungrammatical structure
'which sent' uses a full relative pronoun with an active verb form, but the email was sent by someone, so the passive is required and the pronoun must be dropped in a reduced clause

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The report ___ (prepare) by the finance team is ready for the manager to review.
distractor_rationale: 'preparing' is a present participle indicating an active meaning, but the report did not prepare anything — the passive past participle is needed
'that prepared' inserts a relative pronoun before an active verb, making the report the subject that performed the action, which is semantically impossible here
'has prepared' is a finite present perfect verb form, which cannot function as a reduced relative modifier before 'by the finance team'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The photographs ___ (take) during the school trip last month will be displayed in the main hall next week.
distractor_rationale: 'taking' is a present active participle, implying the photographs were taking something, which is semantically impossible
'that taken' incorrectly places a relative pronoun directly before a past participle without an auxiliary, producing an ungrammatical reduced clause
'were taken' is a full finite passive verb phrase, which turns the modifier into a main clause, leaving the sentence without a main verb

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Most of the furniture ___ (damage) in the flood has already been replaced by the building owners.
distractor_rationale: 'damaging' is a present active participle meaning the furniture was causing damage to something else, which contradicts the context
'that was damaging' uses a full active relative clause, implying the furniture was actively damaging something, which is the wrong meaning and the wrong structure
'which damaged' introduces a full active relative clause where the furniture is the agent of damage, contradicting the passive meaning required by the context

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The new safety procedures ___ (introduce) by the management team last quarter have significantly reduced the number of workplace accidents.
distractor_rationale: 'introducing' is a present active participle, implying the procedures were actively introducing something else, which is semantically impossible and structurally incorrect for a passive meaning
'that introduced' uses a full active relative clause making the procedures the agent of introducing, but the procedures were introduced by the management team, so the passive reduced form is required
'being introduced' expresses an ongoing passive action at the time of reference, but the procedures were completed and already in place last quarter, so the simple past participle is needed

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The email ___ by the manager yesterday has not been read by anyone yet.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'sending' signals active meaning, but the email does not send itself — passive past participle is required
'that sending' incorrectly keeps the relative pronoun and uses the present participle, producing an ungrammatical reduced form
'that sent' incorrectly retains 'that' before the participle — reduced relative clauses drop the relative pronoun and auxiliary

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The report ___ last month showed that sales had increased by fifteen percent.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'publishing' implies the report is actively publishing something, which is not the intended passive meaning
'that was publishing' uses a full active progressive relative clause, which contradicts the passive meaning required here
'that published' uses an active relative clause, but the report does not publish itself — a passive construction is needed

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The cake ___ by Anna for the party looked beautiful and tasted even better.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'making' would mean the cake is actively making something, which is semantically impossible
'that making' combines the relative pronoun with a present participle, producing an ungrammatical string
'that was making' is an active progressive relative clause implying the cake was making something, which is nonsensical

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The new bridge ___ over the river last year has already become an important route for commuters.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'building' implies an active meaning — the bridge is not building anything, so the passive past participle is required
'that built' uses an active relative clause, but the bridge did not build itself — a passive form is needed
'that was built' is a grammatically correct full passive relative clause, but it is not a reduced form and therefore does not fit the gap, which requires only the participle

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The patients ___ to the hospital after the accident were all treated within two hours.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'taking' implies the patients were actively taking someone somewhere, which contradicts the passive meaning
'that taken' incorrectly retains the relative pronoun 'that' before the past participle, making the clause ungrammatical
'who took' is an active relative clause suggesting the patients took someone to the hospital, which reverses the intended meaning

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The photographs ___ during the school trip will be displayed in the main hall next week.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'taking' implies the photographs are actively taking something, which is semantically impossible
'that were taking' is an active progressive relative clause, suggesting the photographs were capturing something themselves, which is nonsensical
'that take' is an active present relative clause, which incorrectly implies the photographs habitually take something

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The proposal ___ by the finance team earlier this week needs to be approved before Friday.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'preparing' implies the proposal is actively preparing something, which makes no sense in this context
'that preparing' incorrectly retains the relative pronoun and uses a present participle, creating an ungrammatical structure
'that has prepared' uses an active present perfect relative clause, implying the proposal itself has prepared something, which is illogical

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The historical documents ___ in the library's archive for over a century were finally made available to the public.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'storing' implies the documents are actively storing something, which contradicts the passive meaning required
'that storing' combines the relative pronoun 'that' with a present participle without an auxiliary, producing an ungrammatical string
'which storing' similarly retains the relative pronoun 'which' before a bare present participle, which is ungrammatical in both full and reduced relative clauses

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The new software ___ specifically for small businesses has helped many local shops manage their accounts more efficiently.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'designing' implies the software is actively designing something, which is semantically wrong in this passive context
'that designed' is an active relative clause suggesting the software designed something, but the software itself was designed by someone — passive meaning is required
'which has designed' is an active present perfect relative clause implying the software has designed something, which misrepresents the intended passive relationship

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The survey results ___ from over two thousand participants across different age groups suggest that people prefer shorter working hours.
distractor_rationale: present participle 'collecting' implies the results are actively collecting something, which reverses the passive relationship — the results were collected, not collecting
'that were collecting' is an active progressive relative clause implying the results were in the process of collecting something, which is semantically impossible
'which collect' is an active present relative clause implying the results habitually collect something, which contradicts the required passive meaning

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The report writing by the manager was sent to all the team members yesterday.
content.error_sentence: The report writing by the manager was sent to all the team members yesterday.
content.corrected_sentence: The report written by the manager was sent to all the team members yesterday.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The photos taking at the school event last Friday are now on the notice board.
content.error_sentence: The photos taking at the school event last Friday are now on the notice board.
content.corrected_sentence: The photos taken at the school event last Friday are now on the notice board.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The package that delivered to our office this morning contained some important documents.
content.error_sentence: The package that delivered to our office this morning contained some important documents.
content.corrected_sentence: The package delivered to our office this morning contained some important documents.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The instructions giving to us before the exam were very clear and easy to follow.
content.error_sentence: The instructions giving to us before the exam were very clear and easy to follow.
content.corrected_sentence: The instructions given to us before the exam were very clear and easy to follow.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The car parking outside the building without a permit was towed away by the police.
content.error_sentence: The car parking outside the building without a permit was towed away by the police.
content.corrected_sentence: The car parked outside the building without a permit was towed away by the police.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The new policy that introduced by the director last month has already changed the way we handle customer complaints.
content.error_sentence: The new policy that introduced by the director last month has already changed the way we handle customer complaints.
content.corrected_sentence: The new policy introduced by the director last month has already changed the way we handle customer complaints.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The feedback collecting from customers over the past three months shows that most people are satisfied with the service.
content.error_sentence: The feedback collecting from customers over the past three months shows that most people are satisfied with the service.
content.corrected_sentence: The feedback collected from customers over the past three months shows that most people are satisfied with the service.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The application forms that submitting online before the deadline will be reviewed by the admissions team next week.
content.error_sentence: The application forms that submitting online before the deadline will be reviewed by the admissions team next week.
content.corrected_sentence: The application forms submitted online before the deadline will be reviewed by the admissions team next week.
